title: FastGrasp: Learning-based Whole-body Control Method for Fast Mobile Grasping
abstract: We propose FastGrasp, a learning-based framework that integrates grasp guidance, whole-body control, and tactile feedback for mobile fast grasping. Our two-stage reinforcement learning strategy first generates diverse grasp candidates via conditional variational autoencoder conditioned on object point clouds, then executes coordinated whole-body motion to approach and grasp objects while in motion.
keyInsights: 首次将全身控制(底盘+手臂+手部)与移动抓取统一在一个端到端学习框架中
基于物体点云的CVAE生成多样化抓取候选姿态
触觉反馈闭环实现抓取力度的自适应调节
两阶段RL策略：先学抓取候选生成，再学协调执行
在快速移动场景中实现鲁棒抓取
methodology: FastGrasp consists of three modules: (1) Grasp Candidate Generation: a CVAE conditioned on object point clouds produces diverse 6-DoF grasp poses; (2) Whole-body Controller: a reinforcement learning policy coordinates base locomotion, arm trajectory, and hand preshaping to approach the target smoothly; (3) Tactile Feedback: tactile sensors on fingertips provide closed-loop force control during the final grasp phase. Training follows a two-stage curriculum: stage 1 trains the CVAE on a large dataset of grasp-object pairs; stage 2 trains the whole-body policy with the frozen CVAE providing grasp targets.
impact: 为移动操作机器人的快速抓取提供了端到端学习方案，触觉反馈闭环提升了实用性
